perturbation.original_text: 1.5 “Confidential Material ” means all information, in any form or medium , known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is not known to the general public, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 “Confidential Material ” means all information, in any form or medium, known or used by City or an Affiliate of the City which is generally known to the public but is still considered confidential under this agreement, including, but not limited to, the know -how, trade secrets, strategic plans, technical information, product information, supplier information, customer information, financial information, marketing information and information as to business opportunities, methods and strategies and research and development of the City and its Affiliates. If and to the extent any Confidential Material is included in any report, ass essment, diagram, memorandum or other document or copied or reproduced in any other form or medium, such report, assessment, diagram, memorandum, document or Confidential Material in such other form or medium will be deemed to be Confidential Material.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Confidential Material' in section 1.5 to include information 'generally known to the public but still considered confidential under this agreement,' it directly contradicts section 4.1(c), which states that the agreement imposes no obligation on the recipient if the material 'has become general public knowledge through no act or fault on the part of the Recipient.' This creates an ambiguity about whether publicly known information is protected or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.0 Exceptions to Confidentiality Oblig ations 4.1 This Agreement imposes no obligation upon the Recipient with respect to the City’s Confidential Material received hereunder that (a) the Recipient can promptly demonstrate with documentary evidence was already legitimately known to the Recipient without a duty of confidentiality prior to the disclosure thereof by the City, (b) is lawfully received by the Recipient from a third party, other than a supplier introduced to the Recipient by the City, without a duty of confidentiality, (c) has become general public knowledge through no act or fault on the part of the Recipient or the Recipient’s Team , or (d) the Recipient can promptly demonstrate with documentary evidence was independently developed by or for the Recipient without the use of any Confidential Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 1.5 redefines 'Confidential Material' to include information 'generally known to the public but still considered confidential under this agreement.' This directly conflicts with section 4.1(c), which states that the agreement imposes no obligation if the material 'has become general public knowledge through no act or fault on the part of the Recipient.' The change creates a contradiction because if something is generally known to the public (4.1(c)), it should not be considered confidential (1.5), making the agreement ambiguous and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The Recipient will use Confidential Material onl y as strictly required for the Purpose and only in the manner and upon the terms specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The Recipient may use Confidential Material as reasonably interpreted by the recipient for the Purpose and in a manner that does not explicitly violate the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will use only as strictly required' to 'may use as reasonably interpreted' in section 3.1 introduces ambiguity. The original text mandates strict adherence to the defined purpose, while the modified text allows for recipient interpretation, creating a contradiction. This change contradicts the clause in section 3.3(a), that states '(a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in section 3.1 changes the requirement from 'will use only as strictly required' to 'may use as reasonably interpreted', which introduces ambiguity and allows the recipient to interpret the usage of Confidential Material. This directly contradicts section 3.3(a), which states that the Recipient will '(a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein'. The original text mandates strict adherence to the defined purpose, but the modified text allows for recipient interpretation, creating a conflict that fits the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 The Recipient will endeavor to hold and keep, and will encourage the Recipient’s Team to hold and keep, the Confidential Material with a reasonable degree of care. Specifically, the Recipient will (a) attempt to prevent unauthorized access, reproduction, disclosure, or use, (b) disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient’s Team as deemed appropriate, (c) make reasonable efforts to avoid copying or reproducing Confidential Material beyond what is strictly necessary, and (d) promptly notify the City of any known unauthorized activities.
perturbation.explanation: The modified language replaces strict obligations with softer, encouragement-based actions. Changing 'will hold and keep' to 'will endeavor to hold and keep' and 'prevent' to 'attempt to prevent' weakens the confidentiality requirement. Also, changing '(b) disclose Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose' to '(b) disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient’s Team as deemed appropriate' creates ambiguity on who gets the data. This directly contradicts the purpose of Section 3.2 which is 'The Recipient will deal in utmost good faith with the City in its use of the Confidential Material provided by the City.' since there is less security to the data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 The Recipient will deal in utmost good faith with the City in its use of the Confidential Material provided by the City.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the confidentiality requirements by replacing strict obligations with softer language, such as changing 'will hold and keep' to 'will endeavor to hold and keep' and 'prevent' to 'attempt to prevent'. Also, changing '(b) disclose Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose' to '(b) disclose Confidential Material to the Recipient’s Team as deemed appropriate' creates ambiguity on who gets the data. This contradicts Section 3.2, which states, 'The Recipient will deal in utmost good faith with the City in its use of the Confidential Material provided by the City,' because the weakened security measures and increased ambiguity undermine the requirement of utmost good faith in handling the confidential material.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 All Confidential Material i s provided on an “as is” basis, and all representations and warranties, express or implied, including as to its accuracy or completeness, fitness for purpose, merchantability, and non -infringement, are hereby disclaimed.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 All Confidential Material is provided on an “as is” basis. While all representations and warranties, express or implied, are generally disclaimed, the City warrants that the Confidential Material is, to the best of its knowledge, accurate and complete for the intended Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'the City warrants that the Confidential Material is, to the best of its knowledge, accurate and complete for the intended Purpose,' it directly contradicts the initial disclaimer of all warranties, introducing confusion about the reliability of the provided material. This change contradicts the beginning of Section 6.1 'All Confidential Material i s provided on an “as is” basis, and all representations and warranties, express or implied, including as to its accuracy or completeness, fitness for purpose, merchantability, and non -infringement, are hereby disclaimed.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 All Confidential Material is provided on an “as is” basis. While all representations and warranties, express or implied, are generally disclaimed, the City warrants that the Confidential Material is, to the best of its knowledge, accurate and complete for the intended Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a warranty regarding the accuracy and completeness of the Confidential Material, which directly contradicts the initial 'as is' provision and the disclaimer of all warranties, including those related to accuracy and completeness, as stated in both the original and contradicted sections. The added warranty creates a clear inconsistency in the terms governing the Confidential Material, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1 The Recipient acknowledges and agrees with the City that (a) the secrecy of the Confidential Material is of the utmost importance to the City, and the Confidential Material is of a sensitive and confidential nature such that monetary damages alone may be inadequate to protect the City’s interests against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement (b) the covenants and conditions of this Agreement are reasonable and necessary for the protection of the City’s business and security and all defences to the s trict enforcement thereof by the City are hereby waived by the Recipient to the fullest extent permitted by law, and (c) a violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement will result in immediate and irreparable damage to the City, and so the City wil l, in addition to any other rights to relief, be entitled to the remedies of specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief in respect of any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, without proof of actual damages or the inadequacy of monetary damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1 The Recipient acknowledges and agrees with the City that (a) the secrecy of the Confidential Material is of considerable importance to the City, however, monetary damages may be adequate to protect the City’s interests against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement (b) the covenants and conditions of this Agreement are reasonable and may be necessary for the protection of the City’s business and security, defenses to the strict enforcement thereof by the City are not waived by the Recipient to the fullest extent permitted by law, and (c) a violation of some of the provisions of this Agreement may result in damage to the City, and the City will be entitled to remedies, however the remedies are not assured.
perturbation.explanation: By softening the acknowledgement of the importance of Confidential Material and the availability of remedies to the City, this change contradicts the section itself which is establishing an acknowledgement and agreement of the recipient that the 'secrecy of the Confidential Material is of the utmost importance to the City' and that a violation of the agreement 'will result in immediate and irreparable damage to the City'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 The Recipient acknowledges and agrees with the City that (a) the secrecy of the Confidential Material is of considerable importance to the City, however, monetary damages may be adequate to protect the City’s interests against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement (b) the covenants and conditions of this Agreement are reasonable and may be necessary for the protection of the City’s business and security, defenses to the strict enforcement thereof by the City are not waived by the Recipient to the fullest extent permitted by law, and (c) a violation of some of the provisions of this Agreement may result in damage to the City, and the City will be entitled to remedies, however the remedies are not assured.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the certainty and strength of the original agreement concerning the confidentiality of material and available remedies. Specifically, it softens the acknowledgement of the importance of Confidential Material, suggesting that monetary damages 'may be' adequate instead of 'may be inadequate', and states that remedies are 'not assured'. This directly contradicts the original intent of Section 7.1, which emphasizes the 'utmost importance' of secrecy and the 'immediate and irreparable damage' resulting from violations, where remedies would be assured.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Upon the request at any time of the City, the Recipient will promptly destroy all Confidential Material and any copies or reproductions thereof in the Recipient ’s possession or under its control or in the possession or under the control of any of the Recipient ’s Team , and will certify in writing such destruction or return of all Confidential Material. If compliance with the foregoing would violate any applicable law or regulation, then such information may be retained pr ovided that it is not used for any purpose other than to evidence the Recipient ’s compliance with such law or regulation, and such retained information must be maintained in confidence as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Upon reasonable notice from the City, the Recipient will make a good faith effort to destroy or return Confidential Material. The Recipient is not required to destroy or return Confidential Material if it is needed for legal or regulatory compliance, or for internal record-keeping purposes. The recipient is not responsible for the destruction of data on devices that are no longer in their possession.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces ambiguity around the destruction or return of confidential material. By changing 'Upon the request at any time of the City, the Recipient will promptly destroy' to 'Upon reasonable notice from the City, the Recipient will make a good faith effort to destroy', weakens the recipient's obligation. It also removes the clause that all retained information must be maintained in confidence, and states that, 'The recipient is not responsible for the destruction of data on devices that are no longer in their possession.' This contradicts clause 3.3 which states 'The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 The Recipient will hold and keep, and will ensure that all of the Recipient ’s Team will hold and keep, the Confidential Material in confidence and in trust for the City, using at least the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, as the Recipient uses to protect its own similar confidential information of like importance, and will, (a) prevent any access, reproduction, disclosure or use of the Confidential Material not expressly authorized herein, (b) disclose the Confidential Material only to those of the Recipient ’s Team who have a definable need to know such information for Purpose, provided that such Recipient ’s Team are bound by a confidentiality agreement with the Recipient no less restrictive than this Agreement, and in the event the employment or appointment of any such person is terminated, the Recipient agrees to use its best efforts to recover any of the Confidential Material in such person’s custody or control. The Recipient will be responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of all or part of the Co nfidential Material or any act in contravention of this Agreement by a person to whom such Confidential Material was given by the Recipient as if the disclosure were made or the act performed directly by the Recipient , (c) not, and will ensure that each of the Recipient ’s Team will not, copy or reproduce any of the Confidential Material, except as strictly neces sary in order to carry out the Purpose, and (d) promptly notify the City in writing of any unauthorized copying, reproduction, use or disclosure of the Confidential Material of which the Recipient is or becomes aware, and such notice will include a detailed description of the circumstances of the copying, reproduction, use or disclosure and the parties involved.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the recipient's obligation to destroy confidential material by changing 'Recipient will promptly destroy' to 'Recipient will make a good faith effort to destroy'. This creates a contradiction with clause 3.3, which holds the recipient responsible for all damages arising from any disclosure of Confidential Material, as if the disclosure was made directly by the Recipient, regardless of whether the data is on devices no longer in their possession. The new text also adds 'The recipient is not responsible for the destruction of data on devices that are no longer in their possession', which directly contradicts the liability clause in 3.3. Therefore, the modifications introduce a strong in-text contradiction, making enforcement unclear.
